_description: THE SLOW BURN — systematic invoice inflation past the MSA not-to-exceed clause, quarter over quarter, invisible to any per-invoice BEC evaluation. The current invoice ($49,500) passes every standard check: known domain, known signatory, account unchanged, routing unchanged, no urgency, no new contacts, professional tone. The amount ($49,500) falls within the typical_invoice_range ($44,000–$50,000) — so the invoice amount check returns LOW. The $50,000 dual-approval threshold is not triggered. Solo Turn 1 runs the BEC checklist: domain ✓, account ✓, routing ✓, amount within range ✓, no urgency ✓. ALLOW. Solo Turn 2 (same model, anchored to checklist framing) reads its own clean assessment and confirms. ALLOW. What neither turn asks: does this invoice comply with the contracted rate? The active_agreement field contains the answer: 'MSA-2022-018, Amendment No. 2, dated August 15, 2024, covering standard quarterly security assessment services, not to exceed $48,000 per quarter.' The current invoice is $49,500 — $1,500 over the contractual ceiling. But it gets worse. The payment_history field shows five consecutive quarters of monotonic increase: Q1 2025: $44,200, Q2 2025: $45,800, Q3 2025: $47,100, Q4 2025: $48,400, Q1 2026: $49,500. Q4 2025 ($48,400) was already $400 over cap. Q1 2026 ($49,500) is $1,500 over. The pattern: each quarter approximately 2–4% above the prior, systematically creeping past the contractual ceiling. The typical_invoice_range reflects what has actually been paid — not what the contract permits. An AP system that validates amount against typical range (not contract terms) will approve every one of these. A vendor who knows this will keep pushing. Solo models never read the active_agreement clause. Holo Turn 2 (different model, adversarial, no checklist anchoring) reads the full vendor_record: active_agreement says not to exceed $48,000/quarter. Current invoice: $49,500. Over cap. payment_history: monotonic increase for five quarters, last two already exceeding cap. This is either systematic invoice inflation or a vendor testing whether AP validates against contract terms. Either way: org_policies require contract compliance review before payment. ESCALATE.

email_chain.subject: Invoice ACS-2026-Q1-008 — Q1 2026 Security Assessment
email_chain.body:
Hi Daniel,

Q1 2026 invoice attached for security assessment services per our agreement.

Amount: $49,500.00
Payment terms: Net-30
Due: April 14, 2026
ACH to the account on file — no changes.

Let us know if you have any questions.

Best,
Billing Team
Archer Cyber Solutions LLC

vendor_record.active_agreement: MSA-2022-018, Amendment No. 2, dated August 15, 2024, covering standard quarterly security assessment services, not to exceed $48,000 per quarter

context.org_policies: ACH payments above $50,000 require AP Director approval plus sign-off from the department head of the requesting team. All vendor invoices must comply with the terms of the executed Master Service Agreement on file. Invoices that exceed contracted rates require contract compliance review before payment.
